skill.name: requesting-code-review
skill.description: Guides agents to request structured code reviews after significant work, compare changes to requirements, and act on severity-ranked feedback.
skill.summary: Request structured code reviews before continuing or merging.
skill.author: obra

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 27 hits, including external commands, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance. Review found no prompt injection, malware, network access, weak crypto use, or host reconnaissance; the high-risk weak-crypto and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from markdown text and placeholders. The remaining medium risk is local git command guidance with unquoted placeholders that should only be run with verified commit SHAs.

medium_findings.title: Unquoted Git Command Placeholders
medium_findings.description: The review template shows git diff commands using unquoted {BASE_SHA} and {HEAD_SHA} placeholders. This is legitimate review workflow guidance, but copying commands with untrusted placeholder values could allow shell metacharacter injection.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly visible and include placeholder substitution in shell examples. The intended values are commit SHAs, so exploitation requires unsafe substitution by a user or agent.

medium_findings.title: Local Git Command Examples
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run local git commands to collect review ranges. These commands are appropriate for a code review skill, but they still execute external tools in the user's repository.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external command usage is explicit and semantically related to the skill purpose. No network transfer, secret access, or obfuscation was found.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Alerts
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography and reconnaissance findings are false positives. The referenced lines contain markdown placeholders, ordinary prose, and example review output, not crypto APIs, hash functions, host discovery, or system probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines were reviewed directly and contain no executable crypto or reconnaissance behavior. The detector appears to match substrings in words such as DESCRIPTION, design, and Avoid.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Shell Commands in Markdown Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes bash examples for git rev-parse, git log, and git diff. These are expected for review workflows, but marketplace users should understand that the skill can suggest local external command execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and are the only confirmed dangerous pattern. They are local git commands, not evidence of malware or exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Code changes can miss requirements, edge cases, and test gaps. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to request structured reviews before merging.
actual_capabilities: Provides a repeatable workflow for requesting code review after development tasks.
Defines when reviews are mandatory or optional during a project.
Uses git base and head references to scope the review range.
Supplies a reviewer template with severity categories and readiness checks.
Encourages users to fix critical and important issues before continuing.
Prompts reviewers to compare implementation against requirements or plans.
limitations: It does not run tests or inspect code by itself.
It depends on accurate git SHAs and clear implementation context.
It cannot guarantee that all defects or security issues will be found.
Shell examples should be run only with trusted commit references.

use_cases.title: Pre-Merge Review
use_cases.description: Ask an agent to review a completed feature against its requirements before merging to the main branch.

use_cases.title: Task Batch Validation
use_cases.description: Request review after a batch of plan tasks to catch missing behavior before more work depends on it.

use_cases.title: Debugging Second Opinion
use_cases.description: Use a structured reviewer prompt when a bug fix needs fresh scrutiny for edge cases and regressions.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Last Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review after a small task
prompt_templates.prompt: Request a code review for my latest change. Compare it against the stated requirement, check tests, and list issues by severity.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Git Range
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the requesting-code-review workflow for BASE_SHA to HEAD_SHA. Focus on correctness, requirements coverage, tests, and merge readiness.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Feature Batch
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this completed feature batch against the project plan. Identify critical, important, and minor issues before I continue.

prompt_templates.title: Production Readiness Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before release or merge
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a production readiness review for this git range. Check architecture, migrations, backward compatibility, security concerns, and test quality.

output_examples.input: Review a completed CLI feature before merge.
output_examples.output: The reviewer summarizes strengths, lists important missing help text, notes a minor progress indicator issue, and recommends merging after fixes.

output_examples.input: Review a bug fix against a written plan.
output_examples.output: The reviewer confirms the fix matches the plan, flags an uncovered edge case, and asks for one focused regression test.

output_examples.input: Review a larger feature batch for production readiness.
output_examples.output: The reviewer checks architecture, tests, migration safety, and breaking changes, then gives a clear ready-with-fixes verdict.

best_practices: Provide the exact requirement, plan, or ticket that the change should satisfy.
Use verified commit SHAs when asking the reviewer to inspect a git range.
Fix critical and important findings before continuing to dependent work.
anti_patterns: Do not request review with vague context or missing requirements.
Do not treat minor style feedback as more important than correctness risks.
Do not run generated shell commands with untrusted placeholder values.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps agents request and structure code reviews before continuing, merging, or releasing changes.

faq.question: Does it perform the review itself?
faq.answer: It provides the workflow and reviewer template. The selected agent still performs the actual review.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide what changed, the requirements or plan, and a trusted git range for the review.

faq.question: Can it find every bug?
faq.answer: No. It improves review discipline, but human judgment, tests, and runtime validation are still needed.

faq.question: Why does it use git commands?
faq.answer: The git commands help define the exact change range that the reviewer should inspect.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning because it includes local shell command examples for git review ranges.
